title: Allantodia allantodioides
taxonomy: Allantodia allantodioides is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It is native to parts of Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. The classification of Allantodia allantodioides is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Athyriaceae. This fern is typically found growing in shaded, humid environments, often near streams or waterfalls. Its classification as a member of the Athyriaceae family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other similar fern species.

intro: Allantodia allantodioides is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Allantodia allantodioides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
